mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% and remains there for at least one month, the zero lower bound constraint becomes binding and equity risk premiums compress. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors rotate into equities seeking yield, with the effect strongest over 20 trading days before mean reversion.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.19% indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at effective lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A month-over-month increase in UNRATE of 0.3 percentage points or more predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, discretionary spending momentum supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand breadth that single-series proxies miss.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST grow, or the joint signal has lower Rank IC than either series alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. A sustained 3-month decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice profit growth downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 50K month-over-month while UNRATE rises simultaneously, this signals a labor market inflection point that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations reset.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50K AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
regime_hint: labor market inflection or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power than either series independently over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real rates. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, real rates are deeply negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term has lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. When GDPC1 shows quarter-over-quarter growth acceleration above 2% annualized, this signals robust demand momentum that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal with no activation threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure supply-side capacity and utilization. When INDPRO declines while PAYEMS growth remains positive, this signals labor hoarding and inefficient capacity use, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as margin compression becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 2-year rolling window.
